theme.vibe: Earthy & Adventurous, High Trust, Premium Travel

headings.usage: Use tight letter spacing (tracking-tight) for large headings > 32px. Use font-black for hero text, font-normal for elegant sub-headers.

body.usage: Use leading-relaxed for body copy. Left-align dense paragraphs. Never center long blocks of text.

anti_patterns: No centered text for long paragraphs.
Avoid bolding body text unless absolutely necessary.

rules: Use #FDFBF7 for the main background to give an earthy, warm, inviting feel rather than sterile white.
Use #1A3C6E (Deep Royal Blue) for structural elements, heavy text, and primary CTAs.
Use #F97316 (Saffron Orange) sparingly for high-energy accents like 'Book Now' badges or active states.
Use #22C55E for trust indicators (WhatsApp button, verified badges, success states).
Never use pure black or grey. Always tint grey with the primary blue.

layouts.hero: Asymmetrical layout. Large fullscreen image taking 100% height, but the main booking/search card floats at the bottom left or bottom center using 'Crystal Glassmorphism'. DO NOT just center the text in the middle of the image.

grids.bento_destinations: Mode A: 'Tetris' Grid for destinations. Use grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12 gap-8. Make one destination a 'Hero' span (col-span-8 row-span-2), others smaller (col-span-4). Never perfectly symmetrical.
grids.tour_packages: Cardless or minimalistic cards. Use large images, 1px subtle borders, no heavy shadows. Align text to the left.
grids.admin_dashboard: Mode B: High Density Grid. Tight gaps (gap-4). Pure white cards with 1px border on a very faint grey-blue background (#F1F5F9). No heavy drop shadows.

layouts.spacing: Generous Spacing. Use p-8, p-12, or p-24 for section padding. Containers with borders must have internal padding p-6 or p-8.

visual_enhancers.textures: Subtle grain texture on the hero overlay to avoid digital flatness.
motion: Smooth scroll using Lenis (yarn add lenis).
Parallax effect on Hero images and destination cards.
Hover states on tour packages: image scale up 105% slightly, wrapper stays fixed (overflow-hidden).

components.buttons: Pill-shaped (rounded-full) for primary CTAs to feel inviting. Sharp corners (rounded-none or rounded-sm) for form inputs to feel structured.
components.booking_bar: Multi-step horizontal or compact bento style. Don't use standard HTML inputs, use Shadcn Select/Popover with custom styling.
components.whatsapp_fab: Fixed bottom-right, pulsating ring effect using Accent color (#22C55E). Z-index 50.

3d_libraries.reason: For a travel booking site, high-fidelity 2D imagery (photos of destinations) builds more trust and emotional connection than 3D models. Parallax and smooth transitions will provide the 'premium' feel instead.

hero.description: Lone hiker looking at Himalayas. Perfect for the main Yatrika Hero image.

destinations.description: Hikers in snow-capped mountains (Tibet/Himalayas). For North India destination card.
Umaid Bhawan Palace in Jodhpur. For West India / Heritage destination card.
Udaipur City Palace. For Rajasthan packages.
Red and white boat near beige building. For Kerala or lake destinations.

about_us.description: Man standing on top of mountain. Use for 'About Yatrika' section.

gallery.description: A view of a building through a window.

accessibility.contrast: Text over hero images MUST have a gradient overlay (black/50 to transparent) or a text-shadow. Normal text must meet 4.5:1 ratio against #FDFBF7.
accessibility.focus: focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#F97316] focus:outline-none on all inputs and buttons.
accessibility.motion: Use prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable parallax for sensitive users.

instructions_to_main_agent: Use @phosphor-icons/react for icons, matching the 'high-end app' aesthetic with Duotone weights.
Install lenis for smooth scrolling (`yarn add lenis`). Initialize it in a Layout or App component.
Use standard Radix UI / Shadcn components for complex forms (Date picker, Select for booking), but override their styles to match the earthy/premium theme. Strip away the Vercel-look.
Do NOT use 'transition: all'. Explicitly define transitions (e.g., transition-transform, transition-opacity).
Ensure all interactive elements have data-testid attributes (e.g., data-testid='book-now-button').
For the admin panel, use pure white cards (#FFFFFF) on a light grey/blue background (#F1F5F9) with high density layouts.
Never apply rounded corners to the main layout wrapper in a way that creates a 'floating app' look on desktop; let the website breathe edge-to-edge.
Ensure the WhatsApp FAB floats above all content with z-index: 50.
Remember that files are .js/.jsx, not .tsx. Do not add TypeScript types in your code.
Use sonner for toasts (located in /app/frontend/src/components/ui/sonner.js or similar).
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💹🔢🏆🥇 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Components MUST use named exports. Pages MUST use default exports.
